meaning: vt.侵蚀,腐蚀,使变化vi.受腐蚀,逐渐消蚀掉
examples: Then over many more years, the Phosphorus-rich rocks of the new land begin to erode and the cycle continues.
The rain will erode the deeds of his life.
The third effect is to erode California's claim to have America's most highly skilled labour force.
However, in light of ongoing state budget and sequestration cuts, mental health services continue to erode.
It took nearly 100 years for labor to see its influence erode in Michigan.
